questions.question: What do students report about the difficulty and focus of Professor Allen's exams?
questions.expected_answer: Exams focus on lecture examples and pattern recognition; midterms contain tricky application-style questions; final is cumulative and heavily weighted (approx. 40%); students recommend attending lectures and practicing homework problems.

questions.question: How many weekly hours do students report for Data Structures?
questions.expected_answer: Approximately 8–12 hours per week on average, with higher hours during assignment-heavy weeks or for students without prior experience.

questions.question: What do students say about grading turnaround in Professor Kim's course?
questions.expected_answer: Turnaround is inconsistent: some students report quick grading (3–5 days) with detailed comments, while others report delays of 2+ weeks and minimal feedback.

questions.question: Do students recommend attending lectures to prepare for exams?
questions.expected_answer: Yes — students recommend attending lectures because exams often draw directly from lecture examples; copying examples and attending review sessions is advised.

questions.question: Which course types do students report as having the heaviest workloads?
questions.expected_answer: Project-heavy courses (software-engineering style) peak at 20+ hours during project weeks; Data Structures and Algorithms are typically 10–15 hours per week; Intro-level courses report lower averages (~6–8 hours).
